title: i18n framework: catalogs, units (metric/imperial), ICU pluralization, locale formatting, completeness check, safe fallback
context: oer-science-labs targets educators in multiple countries and languages. The i18n framework enables localization of both the UI and guide content without requiring code changes for each new locale. A build-time completeness check prevents a half-translated locale from being served; safe fallback to English ensures safety blocks are never served as blank. Depends on code-008 (browse UI must exist before it can be internationalized).
objective: Implement the i18n framework for oer-science-labs covering: message catalogs for UI strings (keyed, not hardcoded), metric-primary with imperial-secondary unit handling, ICU pluralization, locale-aware number and date formatting, a build-time completeness check that fails CI if a locale catalog is missing required keys, and safe fallback to English (never a blank safety block in any locale).
acceptanceCriteria: All UI strings are externalized into locale message catalogs (no hardcoded English strings in UI components)
Metric-primary with imperial-secondary unit display is implemented and switchable per locale or user preference
ICU plural forms are used for messages requiring pluralization; locale-aware number, date, and unit formatting is implemented
Build-time completeness check fails CI when a locale catalog is missing one or more required message keys

Safe fallback to English is implemented for all locales: if a message key is missing, the English string is used rather than showing a blank or key name; safety-block keys are explicitly never blank in any locale
The i18n framework is extensible: adding a new locale requires only adding a catalog file, not code changes
Existing CI gates (schema-validation, exclusion-list, no-telemetry, offline E2E, a11y) continue to pass after this change
output: A pull request adding the i18n framework (message catalogs, unit handling, ICU pluralization, locale formatting, completeness CI check, safe English fallback) to the oer-science-labs repository.
